title: AC Water Leakage Diagnostic
fast_answer: Check for airflow issues or drainage blockage as primary causes of water leakage.

failure_modes.name: Airflow Issues
failure_modes.description: Problems related to restricted or insufficient airflow.

failure_modes.name: Drainage Issues
failure_modes.description: Failures in the condensate drainage system.

failure_modes.name: Electrical Issues
failure_modes.description: Electrical faults causing improper AC operation.

failure_modes.name: Refrigerant / Pressure Issues
failure_modes.description: Problems related to refrigerant levels or pressure abnormalities.

failure_modes.name: Component Failure
failure_modes.description: Malfunctions in specific components of the AC unit.

guided_diagnosis.scenario: If visible water is pooling under the AC unit → check for drainage issues.
guided_diagnosis.action: Inspect the condensate drain line and pan.

guided_diagnosis.scenario: If water leakage occurs when humidity is high → check airflow issues.
guided_diagnosis.action: Inspect and replace the air filter if dirty.

guided_diagnosis.scenario: If the unit shuts off unexpectedly → check for electrical issues.
guided_diagnosis.action: Test the float switch and capacitors.

guided_diagnosis.scenario: If cooling is inadequate → check refrigerant / pressure issues.
guided_diagnosis.action: Measure refrigerant levels and inspect for leaks.

mermaid_diagram:
flowchart TD;
  A[AC Leaking Water] --> B{Check for}
  B --> C[Drainage Issues]
  B --> D[Airflow Issues]
  B --> E[Electrical Issues]
  B --> F[Refrigerant / Pressure Issues]
  C --> G[Inspect drain line]
  C --> H[Inspect condensate pan]
  D --> I[Check air filter]
  D --> J[Check vents]
  E --> K[Test float switch]
  E --> L[Test capacitors]
  F --> M[Measure refrigerant levels]
  F --> N[Inspect for refrigerant leaks]

causes.name: Dirty air filter
causes.why_it_happens: Increased resistance to airflow reduces cooling efficiency.

causes.name: Clogged drain line
causes.why_it_happens: Debris buildup obstructs water flow, leading to overflow.
causes.repair_action: Clear or replace the drain line.

causes.name: Faulty float switch
causes.why_it_happens: A malfunction prevents the system from shutting off if the pan fills.
symptoms_to_confirm: AC continues to run when pan is full

causes.name: Low refrigerant
causes.why_it_happens: Insufficient refrigerant levels can lead to icing on coils and water leakage.

repairs.name: Clear drain line
steps: Use a wet/dry vacuum to clear the clog.

repairs.name: Replace float switch
steps: Remove and replace the faulty switch.
Test the system for proper operation.

repairs.name: Recharge refrigerant
steps: Charge the system to specified pressure.
Check for leaks and verify operation.
